question: Differentiate between Behavioral Metrics and Attitudinal Metrics with examples.
expectedPoints: Behavioral metrics measure what users DO (clicks, page views, time on task, conversion rates)
Attitudinal metrics measure what users THINK and FEEL (surveys, interviews, opinions
Example behavioral: Task Success Rate, Error Rate, Bounce Rate
Example attitudinal: NPS, CSAT, SUS scores

question: Explain the HEART framework for measuring UX with each component.
expectedPoints: Happiness - user satisfaction and attitude (via surveys)
Engagement - level of user involvement (frequency, depth of use)
Adoption - new users starting to use the product
Retention - users returning/continuing to use
Task Success - effectiveness and efficiency in completing tasks

question: Calculate NPS from the following data: Promoters (9-10): 150 users, Passives (7-8): 80 users, Detractors (0-6): 70 users. Total: 300 users.
expectedPoints: Score interpretation: NPS of 27 is considered 'Good' (above 0)

question: What is A/B testing and how is statistical significance determined?
expectedPoints: A/B testing compares two versions (A and B) of a page/app to determine better performer
Users are randomly split between versions
Statistical significance ensures results aren't due to chance (typically ≥95% confidence)
Sample size must be large enough for reliable results

question: Explain the SUS (System Usability Scale) scoring method.
expectedPoints: SUS consists of 10 Likert-scale questions (5 positive, 5 negative)
Each answer scored 1-5, summed to get raw score (0-40)
Multiply by 2.5 to get final score (0-100)
Score >68 is above average, >80 is excellent

question: List and explain any four key UX metrics used in industry.
expectedPoints: NPS - measures loyalty and likelihood to recommend
CSAT - measures satisfaction with specific interaction
CES - measures ease of task completion
Task Success Rate - % of users completing tasks successfully
Conversion Rate - % completing desired action
Bounce Rate - % leaving after single page

question: Explain the AARRR (Pirate Metrics) framework with detailed explanation of each metric and how they contribute to product success.
expectedPoints: Acquisition: How users find your product (channels, sources, costs)
Activation: Users sign up and experience first value (onboarding, initial engagement)
Retention: Users come back repeatedly (returning users, engagement metrics)
Referral: Users recommend to others (viral coefficient, NPS)
Revenue: How you make money (monetization, LTV, ARPU)
Explanation of metrics for each stage and measurement techniques

question: Design a comprehensive UX metrics framework for an e-commerce website. Specify which metrics to track at different stages and how they would be measured.
expectedPoints: Pre-visit/Acquisition: Traffic sources, bounce rate, cost per acquisition
Onboarding/Activation: Sign-up rate, first purchase completion
Engagement: Pages per session, time on site, product views
Retention: Repeat purchase rate, customer lifetime value
Referral: Social shares, review ratings, NPS
Revenue: Conversion rate, average order value, cart abandonment rate
Tools and methods for measurement (analytics, surveys, heatmaps)
How metrics relate to business goals

question: Discuss the importance of UX metrics in the design process. How do they help in iterative design and decision making?
expectedPoints: Provides objective, data-driven insights instead of subjective opinions
Helps identify usability problems early before expensive fixes
Enables A/B testing to compare design alternatives
Tracks progress over time and measures improvements
Connects user experience to business KPIs and ROI
Supports stakeholder communication with quantifiable evidence
Guides prioritization of design improvements
Enables benchmarking against industry standards (e.g., SUS >68)
Supports continuous improvement through measurement cycle

question: A mobile app has 10,000 monthly active users. In a usability test with 100 users, 85 completed the checkout task successfully. The average time on task was 45 seconds with 120 clicks. The app has a 3% conversion rate and NPS of 42. Analyze this data and recommend improvements.
expectedPoints: Task Success Rate: 85% - good but room for improvement (target >90%)
Time on Task: 45 seconds - evaluate against benchmarks
Click count: 120 - high, suggests complex navigation
Conversion Rate: 3% - below e-commerce average (2-5%), needs improvement
NPS: 42 - positive but not excellent (target 50+)
Recommendations: Simplify checkout flow, reduce clicks, improve navigation
Further testing needed to understand user pain points
Prioritize fixes based on impact and effort
